items.why: A classic faux ami. Actuellement looks like 'actually' but means CURRENTLY. To say 'actually' use en fait or en réalité. These false friends regularly cost marks.

items.q: How do you say 'my house' in French?
items.why: Students use mon as default. French possessives agree with the OBJECT's gender, not the owner. Maison is feminine, so MA maison. (Note: mon goes before feminine nouns starting with a vowel - mon amie.)

items.q: Which verbs take 'être' as their auxiliary in the passé composé?
items.correct: Movement/state-change verbs (DR & MRS VAN DER TRAMP) and all reflexive verbs.
items.why: Students forget reflexives. Both groups use être: aller/venir/monter, etc. AND every reflexive verb (je me suis lavé). With être, the past participle agrees with the subject.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my brother'?
items.correct: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.
items.misconception: Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.
items.random_wrong: Je entends bien avec mon frère.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.. “Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which form of 'you' should you use when addressing your French teacher?
items.why: Correct answer: vous. “tu” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many forms does the French adjective 'beau' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur, masc before vowel 'bel')?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 forms. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
